title: metagentiana melvillei
side_effects: Metagentiana melvillei can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach discomfort, which can be caused by ingesting the plant's toxic alkaloids. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can lead to symptoms like rapid heartbeat, convulsions, and even respiratory failure. Long-term exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also cause liver and kidney damage, as well as an increased risk of certain types of cancer. Additionally, improper use of the plant can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching.
